title: pongamia macrophylla
intro: Pongamia macrophylla, also known as the Pongam tree, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and showy yellow flowers. The tree has been utilized for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of pongamol and other bioactive compounds in its seeds, leaves, and bark, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Pongamia macrophylla include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antidiabetic properties, as well as its ability to protect against various diseases. The plant is used in various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and oils, which are employed in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
